report:
## Summary
Jacob, your performance over the last week has been exceptional, achieving 99.2 hours billed, which is 208.8% of your weekly goal. Over the past six weeks, you've consistently maintained high productivity, with four weeks exceeding 90 hours. This trend reflects your strong work ethic and technical skills.

## What's Working
- **High Productivity**: You achieved 99.2 hours billed from April 25 to May 1, the highest in the last six weeks, showcasing your ability to handle a high volume of work efficiently.
- **Consistent Performance**: In the week of April 18 to April 24, you billed 92.7 hours, demonstrating strong performance across multiple days, particularly on Tuesday (19.1h) and Friday (17.0h).
- **Strong Weekday Output**: Your output on weekdays has been impressive, especially on Wednesdays and Thursdays over the past month, with several days exceeding 20 hours.
- **No Open ROs**: Currently, you have no open repair orders, indicating that you are effectively closing out jobs and managing your workload.

## Where to Focus
- **Job Assignment**: There are three open jobs awaiting your assignment, including two high-priority ROs (777092 and 777158). Picking these up promptly can help maintain your productivity and ensure customer satisfaction.
- **Variation in Daily Hours**: While your overall hours are strong, some days show lower productivity, such as Monday (14.6h) during the week of April 25 to May 1. Focusing on maximizing your output on these days can help sustain your overall performance.
- **Follow-Up on High-Priority ROs**: The timing issue (RO 777092) and A/C problem (RO 777158) are critical. Addressing these quickly will help prevent delays and enhance customer service.
- **Weekend Utilization**: You had zero hours billed on Saturdays recently. Exploring opportunities to work on weekends could help boost your overall hours and take advantage of lighter schedules.

## Action Items for This Week
1. Pick up at least one of the high-priority ROs (777092 or 777158) from the shared queue to maintain momentum and address urgent customer needs.
2. Aim to increase your Monday productivity by planning specific tasks or jobs that can be completed efficiently.
3. Consider scheduling time on Saturday to work on additional ROs or follow-ups, especially if the workload allows for it.

## WIP Watchlist
- RO 777092 (timing issue) and RO 777158 (A/C not working) are high-priority jobs that require your attention this week. Prioritize these to enhance customer satisfaction and keep your productivity high.
